info.title: Swarm User-Facing JSON-RPC API
info.description:
Single canonical user-facing API for the Swarm platform. JSON-RPC 2.0 over
HTTP for request/response, JSON-RPC subscriptions over WebSocket for live
streams. One transport, one auth model, one envelope. All operator,
builder, CLI, and dashboard consumers route through this surface.

The spec is Phase-1 in scope (internal — Empire as the only consumer at
spec time) but Phase-2-publishable by construction: no internal jargon in
method names, no [PROPOSED] placeholders that the impl never honors, all
method signatures typed, all shapes consistent across read paths. This
YAML is the OpenRPC source-of-truth — the openrpc.json artifact is
generated mechanically from method_catalog + components.

methods.name: agent.get
methods.description:
Read one agent's identity, current operational status, and references
to its current session and last turn. Returns refs only — full session
and transcript content is read via conversation.* methods. Ownership
boundary: agent.* never returns transcripts.

methods.name: agent.list
methods.description: List declared agents in the loaded bundle, with current operational status.

methods.name: agent.replay_backlog
methods.description: Replay the agent's pending event backlog. Used for recovery after restart or ingress pause.

methods.name: agent.restart
methods.description: Restart an agent's session. Existing in-flight turn is killed; agent re-initializes.

methods.name: agent.send_directive
methods.description: Send a directive (free-form instruction) to a running agent. The runtime resolves the target run before execution: explicit run_id targets an existing nonterminal run; with no run_id, zero active sessions allocate a new run_id, one active session uses that session's run_id, and multiple active sessions fail closed with AMBIGUOUS_RUN_TARGET. The runtime persists platform.agent_directive before BoardStep and all child emissions inherit the resolved run_id.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Optional explicit nonterminal run target. If omitted, the runtime resolves from active sessions or allocates a new run_id.

response.description: Optional acknowledgement or error from the agent.

active_sessions.description: Active sessions that prevent implicit target resolution. A session without run_id is reported without a run_id field and still fail-closes implicit resolution.

run_id.description: Present when ambiguity was caused by an explicit run target combined with incompatible legacy targeting flags.

methods.name: conversation.current_for_agent
methods.description:
Read the agent's current (most-recent active) session. Replaces the
ownership-violating agent.get_session that earlier drafts proposed —
agent.* doesn't own sessions; conversation.* does.

result.name: result
result.description: Returns null if the agent has no active session.

methods.name: conversation.get
methods.description: Read one conversation in full — session metadata plus all turns and turn blocks.

methods.name: conversation.list
methods.description: List conversation summaries (one per session).

methods.name: entity.aggregate
methods.description: Aggregate entity counts grouped by a field.

params.name: group_by
params.description: Field to group by. Defaults to current_state when omitted.

methods.name: entity.get
methods.description: Read one entity in full, including fields, gates, and accumulator state.

methods.name: entity.list
methods.description: List entities with optional filters. Consolidates legacy "instance" and "entity" reads.

methods.name: event.get
methods.description: Read one event with its full delivery and dead-letter detail.

methods.name: event.list
methods.description:
Canonical event store query. Folds dead-letter inspection in via the
has_dead_letter and reason_code filters; no separate dead_letter.*
namespace in v1.

methods.name: event.publish
methods.description:
Canonical low-level event publication and workflow-creation primitive.
Publishes one declared event into the runtime bus. If run_id is omitted,
the server allocates a new run_id, persists the event as that run's
trigger, and returns new_run_created=true. If run_id is provided,
it must identify an existing nonterminal run; missing or terminal
runs fail closed with RUN_NOT_FOUND or RUN_ALREADY_TERMINAL.

The method consumes the same canonical EventBus/store event-publication
owner used by runtime dispatch. Delivery results are read back from the
persisted event_deliveries owner; the API layer must not re-plan
subscribers. This is the canonical primitive; run.start remains only
as a deprecated wrapper for older callers.

params.name: bundle_ref
params.description: Optional assertion that the server is running the expected boot bundle fingerprint.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Existing nonterminal run to inject into; if omitted, runtime allocates and starts a new run.

params.name: emitter
params.description: Optional producer identifier. Defaults to cli-publish:<actor_token_id>.

methods.name: event.subscribe
methods.description:
Live event stream with the same filter shape as event.list. Filter
parity is required by the conventions section. Pagination params
(limit, cursor) and the time-window `until` are NOT accepted here
— subscriptions deliver everything matching the filter live.

params.name: replay_since
params.description: Optional catch-up window. If present, delivers events from this time forward, then continues live. Subject to runtime event retention.

methods.name: health.check
methods.description: Structured health and runtime identity. Operators use this to confirm what server they are talking to and whether it is ready to handle requests.

methods.name: health.ping
methods.description: Cheap aliveness check. Always returns success when the runtime is responsive.

methods.name: health.subscribe
methods.description: WebSocket heartbeat subscription. Notifications carry the same shape as health.check returns.

methods.name: mailbox.approve
methods.description: Approve a pending mailbox item. Triggers the downstream event configured for the item's type by api_specification.conventions.mailbox.approval_event_routes.

params.name: decision_payload
params.description: Additional decision context attached to the downstream event.

methods.name: mailbox.defer
methods.description: Defer a pending mailbox item until a specified time.

methods.name: mailbox.get
methods.description: Read one mailbox item with full payload and decision history.

methods.name: mailbox.list
methods.description: List mailbox items. Filters cover the operator workflow of "find items requiring my attention."

methods.name: mailbox.reject
methods.description: Reject a pending mailbox item with a stated reason. No downstream event is emitted.

methods.name: rpc.unsubscribe
methods.description: Cancel an active WebSocket subscription on the current connection. This is a JSON-RPC protocol method for the subscription transport, not a durable resource mutation.

methods.name: run.continue
methods.description: Resume a paused run.

methods.name: run.diagnose
methods.description:
Interpreted run diagnosis. Returns the run header plus the
operator-state projection from
internal/store/run_operational_status_read_surface.go. The single
canonical owner for "why isn't this run progressing?" — routes the
existing projection through the API instead of having every
consumer recompute it. Today the projection produces
operational_state, blocking_layer, blocking_reason, and
heuristics; richer fields (evidence pointers, suggested_next_action)
are additive owner extensions for a future slice.

methods.name: run.get
methods.description: Cheap canonical read of the run header. Used by lists, hovers, and any consumer that needs run status without paying for diagnostic interpretation.

methods.name: run.list
methods.description: List runs with optional filters. Cursor-paginated.

methods.name: run.pause
methods.description: Pause a run by halting event dispatch. In-flight handlers complete; queued events wait. Run continues on run.continue.

methods.name: run.start
methods.description:
Deprecated wrapper over event.publish retained for older Builder/API callers. New clients should call event.publish directly.
Start a new run by injecting an event into the runtime bus.
Optionally declares a bundle_ref by fingerprint; v1 runtime
requires the fingerprint to match the orchestrator's boot-time
bundle (or the bundle_ref to be omitted, in which case the
boot-time bundle is used). Per-run dynamic bundle loading is
deferred (see deferred_namespaces.bundle_lifecycle).

params.name: run_id
params.description: Caller-provided ID; if omitted, runtime assigns.

methods.name: run.stop
methods.description: Stop an in-progress run. Pending events are abandoned; in-flight handlers complete or roll back per their own semantics.

methods.name: run.subscribe_trace
methods.description:
Live composed trace stream for one run. Each notification carries one
joined trace row (the same RunTraceRow shape returned by run.trace).
Distinct from event.subscribe because trace is a composed view (event
+ delivery + session + turn joined by the runtime), not raw events.

params.name: replay_since
params.description: Optional catch-up window. If present, delivers trace rows from this time forward, then continues live. Subject to runtime trace retention.

methods.name: run.trace
methods.description:
Persisted composed run trace as an ordered list of joined rows. Each
row is anchored on an event and carries delivery, session, and turn
fields where the join finds them. The runtime owns the join — clients
MUST consume rows as-emitted and MUST NOT reconstruct the join from
separate event/delivery/session/turn lists. Backed by the canonical
RunDebugTraceRow surface in internal/store/run_debug_read_surface.go.

methods.name: runtime.incidents
methods.description: Aggregated runtime incidents (errors and warnings grouped by error_code/component for triage).

methods.name: runtime.logs
methods.description: Filtered structured runtime log query.

methods.name: runtime.pause
methods.description: Pause runtime ingress. Queueable event-producing ingress is persisted but not dispatched until runtime.resume; synchronous MCP/tool request-response ingress fails closed while paused. In-flight handlers complete.

methods.name: runtime.resume
methods.description: Resume runtime ingress. Queued event-producing ingress becomes eligible for normal EventBus dispatch exactly once through canonical delivery/idempotency owners.

BlockingLayer.description:
Layer-of-the-system identifier when operational_state is "stalled".
Empty string when state is running or terminal. Source of truth:
ProjectRunOperationalStatus. Current owner emits:
  "" (empty) — state is running or terminal
  "scoring_terminal_outcome" — scoring entities have not reached
    a terminal scoring outcome (shortlisted/marginal/rejected)
  "delivery_lifecycle" — no active deliveries but events exist
Additional values may be added as the operator-state owner gains
more diagnostic semantics. Type stays string (not enum) for
forward compatibility — additive owner extensions must not
require a /v2/ bump.

BundleIdentity.description:
Server bundle identity returned by health.check. Three fields:
fingerprint is the load-bearing identity (sha256 of normalized
contents); workflow_name and workflow_version are human-friendly
labels. Server-local filesystem paths are intentionally NOT
exposed — they leak deployment internals and aren't meaningful
to external Phase-2 consumers. Operators querying "what bundle
is this server running?" get the fingerprint (definitive) and
the labels (informational).

BundleRef.description:
Bundle reference passed by callers to assert "I expect the server
to be running this bundle." Identified by content fingerprint,
NOT server-local filesystem paths — paths cannot be safely
referenced by external Phase-2 consumers, and the runtime's
actual identity check is fingerprint comparison.

v1 runtime requires the fingerprint to match the orchestrator's
boot-time loaded bundle, or the call fails with BUNDLE_MISMATCH.
Per-run dynamic bundle loading (a content-addressable bundle
uploaded or referenced by registry coordinate) is part of the
deferred bundle_lifecycle namespace; v1 BundleRef carries
fingerprint only.

A typical caller flow: call health.check first to read the
server's current bundle.fingerprint, then pass the same
fingerprint in event.publish.bundle_ref or run.start.bundle_ref to assert continuity.

Cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor. Encoded by the runtime; clients MUST treat as opaque and pass back unchanged.

DiagnosisEvidence.description:
Reserved for future owner extension. Currently unreferenced by any
v1 method — the operator-state projection does not yet emit
evidence pointers. Kept in components.schemas as the canonical
shape so that when the owner gains evidence emission (a follow-on
slice), RunDiagnosis can additively reference this type.

kind.description: e.g., event, entity, turn, mailbox_item, dead_letter.

summary.description: Human-readable one-line description of why this evidence supports the diagnosis.

EntityFull.description:
Entity-native shape: typed fields, gates, accumulator state. This
is the destination shape entity.get returns. v1 implementation
may need to translate from the existing instance-shaped readers
in internal/dashboard/server/server.go (which return flat
WorkflowInstance structs without the typed-fields/gates/
accumulated decomposition). The spec commits to the entity-native
shape; implementations are responsible for providing it. A real
entity-read owner may need to land before entity.* methods can
honor this shape, which is why the entity.* methods are
should_have rather than must_have for v1.

accumulated.description: Per-handler accumulator buckets. Map of bucket_key → array of typed items.

fields.description: Typed entity fields per the entity contract. Shape depends on entity_type.

gates.description: Gate state. Map of gate_name → boolean.

deliveries.description: Canonical persisted delivery rows for the published event; empty when the declared event has no subscribers.

downstream_event_id.description: Present only when the decision triggered an event emission (typically on approve).

OpaqueId.description: Opaque identifier. Currently UUID-shaped for runtime-assigned IDs; clients MUST NOT assume UUID shape.

OperationalState.description:
Operational state of a run. Source of truth:
internal/store/run_operational_status_read_surface.go
ProjectRunOperationalStatus. Values are RunTableStatus values
lowercased plus "stalled" as the projection's special
"running-but-not-progressing" state.

result.description: Per-method notification payload. See each subscribe method's notification_schema field.

RunDiagnosis.description:
Run header plus the canonical operator-state projection. Source of
truth: ProjectRunOperationalStatus. Spec exposes exactly what the
owner produces in v1; richer fields (evidence pointers,
suggested_next_action) are additive owner extensions for a future
slice — when the store owner gains them, the spec adds them
additively (no /v2/ bump).

blocking_reason.description:
Free-form reason string when operational_state is "stalled".
Empty when state is running or terminal. Current owner emits:
  "" (empty)
  "terminal_scoring_outcome_missing"
  "no_active_deliveries"
Additional values may be added additively.

heuristics.description:
Free-form diagnostic notes from the projection. Current owner
emits one entry per detected condition, e.g. "dead letters
exist for this run".

ended_at.description: Omitted while run is active; present once status is terminal (completed/failed/cancelled/forked).

error_summary.description: Present only if a top-level error terminated the run.

forked_from_run_id.description: Present only if status indicates a fork.

RunTraceRow.description:
One joined trace row. Every row is anchored on an event; delivery,
session, and turn fields are sparse and present only where the join
finds a corresponding row. Matches the runtime's canonical
RunDebugTraceRow shape (internal/store/run_debug_read_surface.go).
The runtime owns the join — clients MUST consume rows as-emitted
and MUST NOT reconstruct the join from separate event/delivery/
session/turn lists.

ScopeName.description:
Action-resource scopes used for v1 authorization. v1 enforcement
treats every valid token as granting every scope; the enum exists
for forward-compat with Phase 2/3 per-token scope restrictions.
New scopes added by deferred namespaces (e.g., verify:contracts
when verify.* lands, debug:* when debug.* lands) are additive.

Sha256Fingerprint.description: Content-hash fingerprint of a bundle. Stable across paths/worktrees where content is identical.

active_sessions.description: Active sessions that prevent implicit target resolution. A session without run_id is reported without a run_id field and still fail-closes implicit resolution.

run_id.description: Present when ambiguity was caused by an explicit run target combined with incompatible legacy targeting flags.
